calendar.matters:
MOTION To Vacate June 18, 2017 Order Staying Collection Efforts
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Hilliard, Esq. 925-452-4045 for Plaintiff Lenny Semis.
Jennifer McGlone, Esq. 415-200-4983 of Holland Law for Defendant Arkady Berger aka Alec Berger.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ARKADY BERGER'S MOTION To Vacate June 18, 2017 Order Staying Collection Efforts Until Plaintiffs' Motion to Vacate Judgment for Fees is adjudicated.

Defendant Arkady Berger's motion to vacate the June 18, 2017 order staying collection efforts and for additional fees and costs is granted in part. The ruling on the motion to vacate the November 1, 2016 order warrants lifting the stipulated stay. The stipulated stay in the June 18, 2017 order is now lifted and Mr. Berger may pursue collection efforts. Plaintiff Lenny Semis is required to pay $1590 to Mr. Berger. Because plaintiff Lenny Semis' conduct in failing to file a motion to vacate the fees judgment prior to the filing of this motion constitutes frivolous conduct per CCP 128.5, Mr. Berger is entitled to the reasonable fees and costs he incurred in bringing this motion. The amount sought by Mr. Berger far exceeds what is reasonable for this very simple motion. The high end of reasonable for this motion is five hours at $300 per hour and $90 in costs. There was no reason to file reply papers.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Set Aside Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Hilliard, Esq. 925-452-4045 for Plaintiff Lenny Semis.
Jennifer McGlone, Esq. 415-200-4983 of Holland Law for Defendant Arkady Berger aka Alec Berger.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LENNY SEMIS, THE BILS FAMILY TRUST, A CALIFORNIA TRUST's Motion To Set Aside Judgment.

Plaintiffs Lenny Semis and Bils Family Trust's motion to set aside the November 1, 2016 order granting costs and fees as sanctions is denied. The motion was filed at least seven months after the moving parties knew about the November 1, 2016 order and had stated that they would seek to have it vacated. Even if this court had authority to grant this motion more than six months after moving parties acknowledged that they knew about the November 1, 2016 order, the court exercises its discretion to deny the motion on the grounds that moving parties have not acted diligently and there is substantial reason to believe that they had notice of the motion that led to the November 1, 2016 order. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Jennifer McGlone, Esq. of Holland Law, T:415.200.4983, for defendant/moving party;
Brian Hilliard, Esq. of the Hilliard Law Firm, T:415.350.8842, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is stayed and DEFENDANT ARKADY BERGER'S MOTION TO VACATE JUNE 18, 2017 ORDER STAYING COLLECTION EFFORTS AND FOR ADDITIONAL FEES AND COSTS is CONTINUED to February 20, 2018 at 9:30 am to be heard together with Plaintiff's Motion To Set Aside.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
